channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Research Associate in African Democracy and Political Parties at University of Strathclyde (Fully Funded Postdoctoral Opportunity in the UK)
item.dc:creator: Mercy N. Atsuki
item.description:
The Department of Government and Public Policy at the prestigious University of Strathclyde is inviting applications for the position of Research Associate under the groundbreaking European Research Council-funded project titled Political Parties in Africa and Democracy (PPADEM). This exciting postdoctoral opportunity is designed for emerging scholars who are passionate about African politics, democratic governance, political […]

The post Research Associate in African Democracy and Political Parties at University of Strathclyde (Fully Funded Postdoctoral Opportunity in the UK) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Department of Government and Public Policy at the prestigious University of Strathclyde is inviting applications for the position of Research Associate under the groundbreaking European Research Council-funded project titled Political Parties in Africa and Democracy (PPADEM).

This exciting postdoctoral opportunity is designed for emerging scholars who are passionate about African politics, democratic governance, political institutions, elections, comparative politics, and large-scale empirical research. The role offers successful candidates the chance to join an internationally recognized team of scholars working on one of the largest studies of political parties ever conducted globally.

The position is fixed-term for 24 months and provides an excellent platform for early-career academics to build a strong international research profile while contributing to high-impact scholarship on democracy and political parties in Africa.

Salary and Employment Details

Successful applicants will receive:

  • Salary ranging between £37,694 – £46,049
  • Full-time employment (35 hours per week)
  • A fixed-term contract lasting 24 months
  • Opportunities for international collaboration and publication
  • Exposure to leading researchers and institutions in African politics and democracy studies

Application Deadline

29 May 2026

About the PPADEM Project

The Political Parties in Africa and Democracy (PPADEM) project is a major international research collaboration involving:

  • University of Strathclyde
  • Kwame Nkrumah University of Science and Technology
  • University of Bristol
  • University of Reading

The project seeks to understand the major drivers behind democratic decline and democratic progress across Africa by conducting the most comprehensive empirical study of political parties ever undertaken.

Researchers involved in the project aim to answer critical questions such as:

  1. What role do political parties play in democratisation processes in Africa?
  2. Under what conditions do political parties strengthen or weaken democracy?
  3. How can political parties across Africa be supported to defend and expand democratic governance?

This project represents a unique opportunity to contribute to research with real-world political and policy relevance across the African continent.

What the Successful Candidate Will Do

The selected Research Associate will play a central role in the implementation and success of the PPADEM project.

Responsibilities will include:

1. Data Collection and Analysis

The successful candidate will:

  • Collect country-level and organizational-level data on African political parties
  • Analyse and archive political and survey data
  • Support the creation of a large cross-national dataset

2. Survey Design and Research Development

Researchers will:

  • Assist in developing research instruments
  • Help design constituency elite surveys
  • Contribute to party headquarters and branch studies
  • Develop sampling strategies for fieldwork and surveys

3. Pilot Studies and Field Research

The position involves:

  • Supporting pilot studies
  • Testing survey tools and methodologies
  • Participating in data validation and refinement processes

4. Academic Publishing and Conferences

Successful applicants will:

  • Present findings at local and international conferences
  • Participate in workshops and scholarly meetings
  • Contribute to co-authored and sole-authored publications
  • Work toward publication in leading peer-reviewed journals

5. Research Collaboration and Stakeholder Engagement

The Research Associate will:

  • Assist in organizing seminars and project meetings
  • Participate in skills-sharing activities
  • Engage stakeholders and support dissemination of findings
  • Work closely with an international team of senior and early-career scholars

Essential Qualifications

Applicants must possess:

  • A PhD in Political Science or a closely related social science discipline
  • Strong skills in survey design
  • Strong skills in survey data analysis
  • Expertise in comparative politics

The PhD must be completed no later than the date on which the appointment decision is made.

Desirable Qualifications

Preference may be given to candidates with:

  • A publication record appropriate to career stage
  • Experience in research coordination or project management
  • Expertise in elections, public opinion, political parties, or African politics
  • Experience in qualitative research methods such as:
    • Interview research
    • Archival research
    • Case study analysis

Personal Attributes Required

The university is seeking candidates who demonstrate:

  • Ability to work collaboratively in research teams
  • Leadership potential
  • Excellent organizational skills
  • Strong attention to detail
  • Excellent written and spoken English communication skills

Application Requirements

Applicants must complete the university’s online application form and provide the names of two referees.

In addition, candidates must submit the following documents:

Required Documents

  1. Cover Letter
    • Maximum two pages
    • Explain why you are suitable for the role
  2. Curriculum Vitae (CV)
  3. Research Plan
    • Maximum two pages
    • Outline your research strategy for the next two years
    • Explain how your research aligns with the PPADEM project
  4. Writing Sample
    • Working paper
    • Journal article
    • Published chapter or similar academic work
  5. Submit your application documents by registering first HERE.

Interview Information

Formal interviews are expected to take place during the week beginning 15 June 2026.

Why This Opportunity Matters

This position offers a rare opportunity for scholars interested in African democracy, governance, and political institutions to contribute to internationally significant research. Researchers will gain:

  • International academic exposure
  • High-level research experience
  • Opportunities for publication
  • Networking opportunities with leading scholars
  • Experience working on one of the largest political party studies globally

For early-career researchers aiming to build a long-term academic career in comparative politics, democracy studies, or African political research, this role provides exceptional professional development opportunities.

For more details, visit the official University of Strathclyde opportunity page HERE.

Discover more job opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Research Associate in African Democracy and Political Parties at University of Strathclyde (Fully Funded Postdoctoral Opportunity in the UK) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UN Women Women’s Economic Empowerment Internship 2026 in Dakar, Senegal | Fully Funded UN Internship Opportunity for Young Professionals
item.dc:creator: Mercy N. Atsuki
item.description:
The UN Women Women’s Economic Empowerment Internship 2026 in Dakar, Senegal presents an exciting opportunity for graduate students, postgraduate trainees, and recent graduates seeking practical experience in gender equality, development policy, and international cooperation within the United Nations system. Hosted at the UN Women Regional Office for West and Central Africa (WCARO) in Dakar, Senegal, […]

The post UN Women Women’s Economic Empowerment Internship 2026 in Dakar, Senegal | Fully Funded UN Internship Opportunity for Young Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The UN Women Women’s Economic Empowerment Internship 2026 in Dakar, Senegal presents an exciting opportunity for graduate students, postgraduate trainees, and recent graduates seeking practical experience in gender equality, development policy, and international cooperation within the United Nations system.

Hosted at the UN Women Regional Office for West and Central Africa (WCARO) in Dakar, Senegal, this six-month internship is designed for highly motivated individuals who are passionate about women’s economic empowerment, feminist economics, sustainable development, and gender-responsive policymaking in Africa.

The internship offers participants the chance to work directly with experts and policymakers on major development issues affecting women and girls across West and Central Africa. Interns will contribute to research, policy development, communications, advocacy, and regional coordination activities while gaining valuable professional exposure in an international multicultural environment.

About UN Women

UN Women is the United Nations entity dedicated to gender equality and the empowerment of women. Established by the United Nations General Assembly in July 2010, the organization works globally to eliminate discrimination against women and girls while advancing women’s rights, participation, and leadership across all sectors.

UN Women’s Strategic Plan 2026–2029 focuses on addressing systemic inequalities through integrated approaches centered around:

  • Women’s leadership and decision-making
  • Women’s economic empowerment
  • Ending violence against women and girls
  • Women, peace, security, and humanitarian action

The organization operates through regional offices, country offices, and partnerships with governments, civil society organizations, youth groups, and the private sector.

Internship Location and Duration

The internship will take place in Dakar, Senegal, at the UN Women Regional Office for West and Central Africa.

Internship Details

  • Location: Dakar, Senegal
  • Organization: UN Women
  • Department: Women’s Economic Empowerment
  • Duration: 6 Months
  • Schedule: Full-Time
  • Vacancy Type: Internship Programme

Application Deadline

Applicants must submit their applications before:

Deadline: 27 May 2026

Candidates are encouraged to apply early to avoid last-minute technical issues or incomplete submissions.

Overview of the Women’s Economic Empowerment Team

The Women’s Economic Empowerment (WEE) Team at UN Women WCARO collaborates with governments, United Nations agencies, civil society organizations, women’s associations, youth organizations, and private-sector actors to strengthen women’s participation in economic development.

The team focuses on several major thematic areas, including:

1. Women’s Access to Economic Opportunities

The program supports women’s:

  • Employment opportunities
  • Entrepreneurship development
  • Financial inclusion
  • Participation in the green and blue economy

2. Care Economy Transformation

UN Women promotes:

  • Improved care systems
  • Access to childcare and care infrastructure
  • Women-led care enterprises
  • Gender-responsive social protection systems

3. Gender-Responsive Economic Policies

The organization also supports:

  • Macroeconomic policy reforms
  • Gender-sensitive budgeting
  • Inclusive economic frameworks
  • Sustainable economic development strategies

Interns joining this team will gain direct exposure to policy discussions and development programs shaping women’s economic participation across Africa.

Roles and Responsibilities of the Intern

Selected interns will report to the Regional Policy Advisor for Women’s Economic Empowerment and support the daily operations of the WEE team.

Responsibilities include:

Research and Data Collection

Interns will:

  • Conduct targeted research on gender and development issues
  • Collect and analyze data related to women’s economic empowerment
  • Support regional studies and policy reviews

Knowledge and Communication Products

Interns will assist in:

  • Drafting policy briefs
  • Preparing advocacy documents
  • Developing communication materials
  • Producing reports on emerging development issues

Topics may include:

  • Care systems transformation
  • Gender and macroeconomic policy
  • Women in the green economy
  • Innovative financing
  • Care-climate nexus

Administrative and Editorial Support

Responsibilities also include:

  • Drafting reports and meeting minutes
  • Supporting translation work
  • Preparing PowerPoint presentations
  • Assisting with project proposals

Event Coordination

Interns will help organize:

  • Capacity-building webinars
  • Regional workshops
  • Development forums
  • Stakeholder engagement events

Participation in Activities

Interns may participate in:

  • Policy dialogues
  • Gender equality campaigns
  • Regional consultations
  • Women’s empowerment events

Learning Opportunities and Professional Development

This internship provides extensive professional growth opportunities, including:

Participants Will Gain:

  • Advanced knowledge of gender equality frameworks
  • Practical understanding of women’s economic empowerment strategies
  • Experience in international development work
  • Exposure to UN operational systems
  • Professional communication and policy-writing skills
  • Teamwork experience in multicultural settings
  • Networking opportunities with UN agencies and development professionals

Required Qualifications

Applicants must meet at least one of the following educational requirements:

Eligible Academic Status

Candidates must:

  • Be enrolled in a graduate school program
  • Be enrolled in a postgraduate traineeship program
  • Be pursuing a master’s degree or higher
  • Or have graduated within the last two years

Relevant Academic Fields

Preferred disciplines include:

  • Feminist/Gender Economics
  • Gender and Development
  • Development Economics
  • Macroeconomics
  • Related Development Studies

Preferred Experience

Although not mandatory, the following will be considered an advantage:

  • Experience in women’s economic empowerment projects
  • Knowledge of development challenges in Africa
  • Familiarity with West and Central African contexts
  • Understanding of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), especially SDG 5

Required Skills and Competencies

Technical Skills

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Strong analytical and research abilities
  • Knowledge of feminist and gender economics
  • Excellent drafting and writing skills
  • Proficiency in MS Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Teams, and Zoom

Language Requirements

Applicants must have:

  • Excellent written and spoken English
  • Excellent written and spoken French

Personal Competencies

UN Women seeks candidates with:

  • Integrity and professionalism
  • Respect for diversity
  • Accountability
  • Effective communication skills
  • Inclusive collaboration abilities
  • Creative problem-solving skills

Financial Support and Stipend

UN Women provides a stipend to interns who are not receiving financial support from another institution such as a university or scholarship program.

The stipend is intended to help partially cover basic living expenses during the internship period.

Diversity and Inclusion Commitment

UN Women maintains a strong commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion. The organization recruits individuals regardless of:

  • Race
  • Religion
  • Gender identity
  • Sexual orientation
  • Disability
  • National origin
  • Age

The organization also provides reasonable accommodations for applicants requiring support during the recruitment process.

Why You Should Apply

This internship is ideal for young professionals seeking:

  • UN system experience
  • International development exposure
  • Gender policy expertise
  • Professional networking opportunities
  • Research and advocacy experience
  • Practical skills in women’s empowerment programming

Working with UN Women can significantly strengthen your future career prospects in:

  • International development
  • Gender policy
  • Economic empowerment programming
  • Human rights advocacy
  • Public policy
  • Research and academia

How to Apply

Interested candidates should apply through the official UN Women careers portal before the application deadline HERE.

Applicants are advised to:

  1. Complete every section of the application form carefully
  2. Include detailed education and employment history
  3. Prepare supporting documents in advance
  4. Ensure accuracy before submission

To learn more about this opportunity, click HERE.

Incomplete applications may be disqualified during the recruitment process.

Discover more internship opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post UN Women Women’s Economic Empowerment Internship 2026 in Dakar, Senegal | Fully Funded UN Internship Opportunity for Young Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Study at the Prestigious University of Freiburg in Germany: Explore 239 Degree Programmes for International Students
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The University of Freiburg in Germany is offering an extensive range of undergraduate, postgraduate, teacher education, and continuing education degree programmes for students from around the world. With 239 degree programmes across diverse academic disciplines, the university continues to position itself as one of Europe’s leading research and teaching institutions. Known for academic excellence, innovation, […]

The post Study at the Prestigious University of Freiburg in Germany: Explore 239 Degree Programmes for International Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Freiburg in Germany is offering an extensive range of undergraduate, postgraduate, teacher education, and continuing education degree programmes for students from around the world. With 239 degree programmes across diverse academic disciplines, the university continues to position itself as one of Europe’s leading research and teaching institutions.

Known for academic excellence, innovation, interdisciplinary learning, and international collaboration, the University of Freiburg attracts students interested in fields ranging from engineering and environmental sciences to medicine, law, economics, humanities, social sciences, and sustainability studies.

The institution provides opportunities for Bachelor’s, Master’s, State Examination, MBA, and specialized continuing education programmes, including several international double-degree options and English-taught courses.

For international students looking to study in Germany at a globally respected institution with strong research infrastructure and global partnerships, the University of Freiburg offers one of the most comprehensive academic portfolios in Europe.

About the University of Freiburg

The University of Freiburg is one of Germany’s oldest and most prestigious universities, recognized internationally for:

  • Academic excellence
  • Research innovation
  • Interdisciplinary collaboration
  • Sustainability leadership
  • Global partnerships
  • International student engagement

The university offers programmes through multiple faculties including:

  • Faculty of Medicine
  • Faculty of Engineering
  • Faculty of Biology
  • Faculty of Environment and Natural Resources
  • Faculty of Law
  • Faculty of Humanities
  • Faculty of Economics and Behavioral Sciences
  • Faculty of Mathematics and Physics
  • Faculty of Chemistry and Pharmacy
  • Faculty of Theology
  • Faculty of Philology

The university also collaborates with international academic networks and institutions, strengthening opportunities for student exchange, global research, and double-degree programmes.

Why Study at the University of Freiburg?

The University of Freiburg stands out because of its:

1. Extensive Academic Opportunities

Students can choose from:

  • 239 degree programmes
  • Undergraduate and postgraduate options
  • English-taught programmes
  • International double-degree programmes
  • Specialized research-oriented studies
  • Continuing education programmes

The university supports a wide variety of academic interests and career paths.

2. Strong International Reputation

The university is internationally recognized for:

  • Cutting-edge research
  • Innovation-driven education
  • Academic rigor
  • Sustainability research
  • Interdisciplinary collaboration

Students graduate with qualifications respected globally.

3. Research and Innovation Excellence

The university is deeply committed to:

  • Scientific research
  • Climate and sustainability studies
  • Technology and engineering innovation
  • Medical and health sciences
  • Social sciences and public policy research

This makes Freiburg an ideal destination for students interested in research-intensive learning environments.

4. International Double-Degree Opportunities

Several programmes offer international double degrees, enabling students to study across multiple countries and institutions.

Examples include:

  • Applied Political Science
  • Biochemistry and Biophysics
  • Biomedical Sciences
  • Environmental programmes
  • German-French Journalism
  • International Economic Relations
  • Forest Sciences
  • Liberal Arts and Sciences

These programmes provide international academic exposure and global career advantages.

Available Degree Types

The University of Freiburg offers a broad variety of degree structures, including:

Undergraduate Degrees

  • Bachelor of Arts (B.A.)
  • Bachelor of Science (B.Sc.)
  • Polyvalent Dual-Major Bachelor’s Degrees
  • State Examination Programmes

Postgraduate Degrees

  • Master of Arts (M.A.)
  • Master of Science (M.Sc.)
  • Master of Education (M.Ed.)
  • Master of Laws (LL.M.)
  • Master of Business Administration (MBA)

Specialized and Continuing Education Programmes

  • Continuing Education Programmes
  • Research-focused Master’s Programmes
  • Professional postgraduate qualifications

Major Academic Fields Available

The University of Freiburg offers programmes across multiple disciplines.

1. Engineering and Technology

Students interested in engineering and technology can explore programmes such as:

  • Embedded Systems Engineering
  • Microsystems Engineering
  • Sustainable Systems Engineering
  • Computer Science
  • Applied Physics

These programmes prepare students for careers in innovation, technology, and research.

2. Environmental and Sustainability Studies

The university is internationally recognized for sustainability and environmental research.

Available programmes include:

  • Environmental Governance
  • Environmental Sciences
  • Geography of Global Change
  • Hydrology
  • Sustainable Materials
  • Forest Sciences
  • Climate-related research fields

These programmes are highly relevant for students passionate about sustainability and climate action.

3. Medicine and Health Sciences

Students can pursue programmes in:

  • Human Medicine
  • Molecular Medicine
  • Biomedical Sciences
  • Nursing Sciences
  • Midwifery Science
  • Pharmaceutical Sciences
  • Global Urban Health

These programmes combine theoretical learning with practical and research experiences.

4. Social Sciences and Humanities

Available programmes include:

  • Political Science
  • Sociology
  • Social Sciences
  • Anthropology
  • History
  • Philosophy
  • Religious Studies
  • Cultural Studies
  • Media and Cultural Research

These programmes support critical thinking, research, and policy engagement.

5. Economics and Business

The university also offers programmes in:

  • Economics
  • Business Administration
  • Public and Non-Profit Management
  • International Economic Relations
  • Taxation
  • MBA Programmes

These programmes prepare students for leadership roles in business, public administration, and international organizations.

6. Languages, Arts, and Cultural Studies

Students interested in humanities and languages can explore:

  • English and American Studies
  • German Studies
  • French Studies
  • Russian Studies
  • Scandinavian Studies
  • Journalism
  • Musicology
  • Art History
  • Literature Programmes

These programmes encourage intercultural understanding and global engagement.

English-Taught and International Programmes

The University of Freiburg offers multiple English-taught and internationally oriented programmes, making it attractive to students worldwide.

International students can access:

  • English-language courses
  • International double degrees
  • Cross-border academic partnerships
  • Multicultural campus experiences

This global orientation enhances employability and international career prospects.

Admission Types and Entry Requirements

Programmes at the university may have different admission categories:

Open Admission Programmes

Some programmes are available without special admission restrictions.

Restricted Admission Programmes

Certain competitive programmes require:

  • Specific academic qualifications
  • Admission assessments
  • Special prerequisites

Programmes with Special Requirements

Some programmes may require:

  • Language proficiency
  • Prior academic background
  • Subject-specific prerequisites
  • Additional documentation

Applicants should carefully review programme-specific requirements before applying.

Study Duration

Programme durations vary depending on degree type.

Typical durations include:

  • Bachelor’s degrees: 6–8 semesters
  • Master’s degrees: 2–4 semesters
  • State Examination programmes: 8–13 semesters
  • Continuing education programmes: Flexible duration

Winter and Summer Intake Opportunities

The university offers admissions during:

  • Winter Semester
  • Summer Semester

However, some programmes are available only during specific semesters.

Students should confirm intake periods for their preferred programme.

Benefits of Studying at the University of Freiburg

Students enrolled at the University of Freiburg may benefit from:

  • Access to world-class education
  • Research-intensive learning
  • International networking
  • Modern academic facilities
  • Interdisciplinary learning
  • Global partnerships
  • Career development opportunities
  • International double degrees
  • Sustainability-focused education

Student Life in Freiburg

Freiburg is known as one of Germany’s most student-friendly and environmentally conscious cities.

Students can enjoy:

  • A vibrant academic atmosphere
  • International student communities
  • Cultural diversity
  • Sustainable urban living
  • Access to nature and outdoor activities
  • Strong public transportation systems

The city offers an ideal environment for academic and personal growth.

How to Explore Programmes

Students can search for programmes by:

  • Subject area
  • Degree type
  • Faculty
  • Semester
  • Language of instruction
  • Admission type

The university provides extensive programme listings to help students identify the best fit for their academic goals.

Please click HERE to discover all the available programs and other information.

Deadlines vary according to programme. See full details HERE.

Conclusion

The University of Freiburg offers one of the most comprehensive and internationally attractive academic environments in Germany and Europe.

With 239 degree programmes spanning science, technology, sustainability, medicine, business, humanities, social sciences, and engineering, students from around the world can find high-quality educational opportunities tailored to their interests and career aspirations.

The university’s strong commitment to research, innovation, sustainability, and global collaboration makes it an outstanding destination for students seeking internationally respected qualifications and transformative learning experiences.

For students planning to study in Germany, the University of Freiburg represents an excellent opportunity to gain access to world-class education, global networks, and future-ready academic programmes.

Discover more global Scholarship opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Study at the Prestigious University of Freiburg in Germany: Explore 239 Degree Programmes for International Students appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Launch to Work: Africa (L2W) 2026 Employability Programme for Young African Graduates
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Launch to Work: Africa (L2W) 2026 programme, a fully funded employability initiative designed to support young African graduates as they transition from education into the professional world. Developed through a partnership between SOS Children’s Villages Worldwide and Industry Immersion Africa, the Launch to Work: Africa programme seeks to bridge […]

The post Launch to Work: Africa (L2W) 2026 Employability Programme for Young African Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Launch to Work: Africa (L2W) 2026 programme, a fully funded employability initiative designed to support young African graduates as they transition from education into the professional world.

Developed through a partnership between SOS Children’s Villages Worldwide and Industry Immersion Africa, the Launch to Work: Africa programme seeks to bridge the gap between academic achievement and employment opportunities by equipping graduates with workplace skills, professional development support, and internship placements.

Unlike many employability initiatives that focus primarily on STEM graduates, L2W is intentionally inclusive and open to graduates from all academic disciplines.

The programme recognizes that talent exists across every field of study and aims to create pathways into meaningful employment for young Africans from diverse educational backgrounds.

About Launch to Work: Africa (L2W)

Launch to Work: Africa was created to address one of the most significant challenges facing graduates across the continent: transitioning successfully from university to employment.

While many graduates possess academic qualifications, they often lack access to professional guidance, industry exposure, and practical workplace experience that employers increasingly expect.

L2W is designed to help participants build confidence, career clarity, employability skills, and professional readiness through a structured development journey.

The programme focuses on preparing graduates not only to secure opportunities but also to succeed in today’s rapidly evolving workplace.

What Makes the Programme Different?

Many graduate programmes focus heavily on science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) disciplines. However, Launch to Work: Africa takes a broader and more inclusive approach.

The programme welcomes graduates from all educational backgrounds, recognizing that employability and innovation are not limited to a single academic field.

Whether applicants studied:

  • Arts and humanities
  • Social sciences
  • Business
  • Education
  • Technology
  • Communications
  • Health sciences
  • Engineering
  • Or any other accredited discipline

they are encouraged to apply if they are motivated to build their careers.

Programme Structure

The Launch to Work: Africa programme is organized into three key stages designed to support participants throughout their employability journey.

1. Align

The Align phase helps participants develop clarity around their professional identity, career goals, strengths, and personal brand.

Participants will learn how to:

  • Understand their career interests
  • Position themselves professionally
  • Present their skills effectively to employers
  • Build confidence in workplace environments

This stage focuses on helping graduates define who they are professionally and how they can communicate their value in competitive job markets.

2. Launch

The Launch stage focuses on practical innovation and workplace readiness skills needed in modern professional environments.

Participants will gain exposure to:

  • Innovation and problem-solving
  • Workplace communication
  • Professional collaboration
  • Career readiness training
  • Industry expectations
  • Practical employability skills

The programme is designed to prepare graduates for real-world professional challenges rather than only academic success.

3. Employ

The final Employ stage provides participants with supported internship placement opportunities.

Rather than ending with training alone, the programme helps graduates gain direct workplace exposure through internship experiences that can strengthen their resumes and improve long-term employability.

This practical component is one of the programme’s major strengths, offering participants an opportunity to apply their learning in professional settings.

Programme Benefits

Participants selected for Launch to Work: Africa will benefit from:

Fully Funded Participation

The programme is completely free for selected participants, removing financial barriers to career development opportunities.

Professional Identity Development

Graduates receive support in understanding and communicating their professional strengths and career goals.

Workplace Readiness Skills

Participants gain practical innovation and employability skills relevant to today’s job market.

Internship Placement Support

The programme includes a supported internship placement component to provide real-world professional experience.

Career Confidence and Exposure

Participants build confidence, workplace knowledge, and professional networks that can support long-term career growth.

Who Should Apply?

The programme is ideal for:

  • Recent graduates
  • Young African professionals
  • Individuals seeking their first professional opportunity
  • Graduates struggling to transition into employment
  • Young people looking to strengthen their workplace readiness

Applicants from all academic backgrounds are encouraged to apply.

Why This Programme Matters

Youth unemployment and underemployment remain major challenges across Africa, even among university graduates.

Many graduates complete their studies with strong academic credentials but face difficulties entering the workforce due to limited professional exposure, lack of practical experience, or insufficient employability support.

Launch to Work: Africa seeks to address these gaps by helping graduates:

  • Build career confidence
  • Gain workplace skills
  • Improve employability
  • Access internship opportunities
  • Transition successfully into professional careers

The programme’s inclusive approach also challenges traditional assumptions that career development initiatives should focus only on STEM fields.

Application Deadline

Applications for the Launch to Work: Africa (L2W) 2026 programme are open until:

  • 3 July 2026

Interested applicants are encouraged to apply early.

For more information click here 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Launch to Work: Africa (L2W) 2026 Employability Programme for Young African Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Max Planck Institute Opens Research Positions in Education Inequality and Teacher Politics Under the TICA Project
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Max Planck Institute for Research on Collective Goods and the interdisciplinary research group “Teachers, Inequality and Collective Action” (TICA) are inviting applications for multiple academic research positions beginning in Autumn 2026. Led by Dr. Hania Sobhy, the TICA research group examines teachers as central actors in the transformation and restructuring of public education systems […]

The post Max Planck Institute Opens Research Positions in Education Inequality and Teacher Politics Under the TICA Project appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Max Planck Institute for Research on Collective Goods and the interdisciplinary research group “Teachers, Inequality and Collective Action” (TICA) are inviting applications for multiple academic research positions beginning in Autumn 2026.

Led by Dr. Hania Sobhy, the TICA research group examines teachers as central actors in the transformation and restructuring of public education systems worldwide. The project focuses on how educators respond to social inequality, political change, privatization, digitalization, and evolving professional demands within schooling systems.

The initiative is part of the wider EduTrack network — a six-year interdisciplinary research collaboration exploring the future of education across multiple Max Planck institutes.

The newly announced openings provide exceptional opportunities for researchers interested in education policy, inequality, political sociology, teacher mobilization, and comparative education research.

Available Positions

The TICA research group is currently recruiting for the following academic positions:

Researcher Position

  • Application Deadline: 6 June 2026
  • Expected Start Date: Autumn 2026

Postdoctoral Researcher Position

  • Application Deadline: 31 May 2026
  • Expected Start Date: Autumn 2026

Doctoral Researcher Position

  • Application Deadline: 31 May 2026
  • Expected Start Date: Autumn 2026

These positions are designed for scholars seeking to contribute to cutting-edge interdisciplinary research on public education systems, inequality, and collective action.

About the TICA Research Group

The “Teachers, Inequality and Collective Action” (TICA) project investigates how teachers navigate major transformations currently affecting public education systems around the world.

The group studies how educators’ social positions, political beliefs, professional experiences, and institutional contexts shape their views on inequality and influence their actions within education systems.

The project addresses major global concerns related to:

  • Underfunding of public education
  • Privatization of schooling
  • Digital transformation in education
  • Political polarization
  • Challenges to liberal democratic values
  • Educational stagnation and inequality

While education crises in parts of the Global South have long been recognized, the project also examines growing concerns over educational decline and stagnation in parts of the Global North.

Research Focus Areas

The TICA project combines political sociology, education research, and social inequality studies to explore several major themes.

Teachers and Social Inequality

One key area of research investigates how teachers perceive inequalities shaped by:

  • Social class
  • Gender
  • Migration background
  • Language
  • Intersectional inequalities

The project explores how teachers explain these inequalities and what forms of intervention they consider effective.

Collective Action and Teacher Organization

Another major focus area examines teachers’ engagement in collective representation and organization, including:

  • Trade unions
  • Professional associations
  • Alternative organizing structures
  • Collective advocacy efforts

Researchers will explore how professional experiences and political beliefs influence teachers’ participation in collective action.

Educational System Transformation

The project also studies how teachers perceive broader structural changes affecting education systems globally, including privatization, austerity, and technological change.

The research investigates how these perceptions vary across:

  • Professional profiles
  • Social backgrounds
  • Generational cohorts
  • Political ideologies

Methodology and Research Approach

TICA employs an interdisciplinary and mixed-methods research framework combining:

  • Institutional mapping
  • Survey data analysis
  • Qualitative research methods
  • Comparative education research
  • Political and sociological analysis

This approach enables researchers to examine both structural trends and lived educational experiences across diverse contexts.

Regional Focus: Middle East and North Africa (MENA)

The empirical work of the TICA project is anchored in the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) region.

The region offers important comparative insights into how public education systems are being reshaped under conditions of political transition, economic pressure, privatization, and social transformation.

The research group also seeks to foster broader international dialogue on teacher politics, inequality, and collective action beyond the MENA region through workshops, collaborations, and academic exchanges.

About the EduTrack Network

TICA is part of the EduTrack initiative — a six-year interdisciplinary research network funded by the Max Planck Society.

EduTrack explores the future of education through collaborative research involving multiple Max Planck institutes, including:

  • Max Planck Institute for Demographic Research
  • Max Planck Institute for the History of Science
  • Max Planck Institute for Political and Social Sciences

The network is coordinated by Population Europe.

Why These Positions Matter

The restructuring of public education systems is one of the defining global challenges of the modern era. Teachers increasingly operate under conditions shaped by economic inequality, political tension, technological disruption, and changing social expectations.

The TICA project offers scholars an opportunity to contribute to globally relevant research examining how educators understand and respond to these transformations.

Researchers joining the project will become part of a major international academic network while contributing to interdisciplinary debates on education, inequality, governance, and social change.

Application Deadlines

Researcher Position

  • Deadline: 6 June 2026

Postdoctoral Researcher Position

  • Deadline: 31 May 2026

Doctoral Researcher Position

  • Deadline: 31 May 2026

Applicants are encouraged to prepare materials early due to the competitive nature of Max Planck research opportunities.

For more information click here 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Max Planck Institute Opens Research Positions in Education Inequality and Teacher Politics Under the TICA Project appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Young Women in Mind 2026–2028 Grant Programme: Up to £500,000 for Mental Health Services Supporting Young Women in the UK
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Young Women in Mind 2026–2028 funding programme is now open for applications, offering significant grant funding to charities delivering therapeutic mental health support for girls and young women across the United Kingdom. Launched through a collaboration between the Pilgrim Trust, Prudence Trust, and Julia Rausing Trust, this major initiative will invest £5 million over […]

The post Young Women in Mind 2026–2028 Grant Programme: Up to £500,000 for Mental Health Services Supporting Young Women in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Young Women in Mind 2026–2028 funding programme is now open for applications, offering significant grant funding to charities delivering therapeutic mental health support for girls and young women across the United Kingdom.

Launched through a collaboration between the Pilgrim Trust, Prudence Trust, and Julia Rausing Trust, this major initiative will invest £5 million over three years to strengthen and expand mental health services specifically designed for young women aged 14–25.

The programme seeks to address the growing mental health challenges facing young women by supporting organizations that provide accessible, community-based, gender-specific therapeutic services.

About the Young Women in Mind Programme

Young Women in Mind 2026–2028 was developed in response to increasing concern over worsening mental health outcomes among young women in the UK. The programme recognizes that many young women struggle to access timely mental health care that reflects their lived experiences and responds effectively to their needs.

Through this collaborative funding initiative, the participating trusts aim to support charities that already provide therapeutic mental health services and help them:

  • Expand existing services
  • Strengthen operational capacity
  • Adapt programmes to meet growing demand
  • Improve accessibility and inclusivity
  • Deliver timely interventions before mental health challenges become more severe

The programme places strong emphasis on community-based support and trusted environments where young women already seek help.

Funding Details

The Young Women in Mind programme offers substantial multi-year funding for eligible charities.

Grant Size

Successful organizations can receive:

  • Between £200,000 and £500,000
  • Distributed across a three-year period (2026–2028)

This long-term funding model is intended to provide charities with sustainability and flexibility to strengthen service delivery.

What the Funding Covers

The grant funding can support a wide range of operational and programme-related costs.

Eligible expenses include:

  • Project delivery costs
  • Staff salaries and staffing support
  • Professional fees
  • Advocacy activities
  • Learning and evaluation costs
  • Service adaptation and improvement
  • Programme expansion and scaling

The funding is designed to help organizations build stronger and more effective therapeutic mental health support systems for young women.

Programme Focus Areas

The Young Women in Mind initiative specifically supports charities providing gender-specific therapeutic mental health services for girls and young women aged 14–25.

The programme acknowledges that young women often face unique social, emotional, and structural challenges that require specialized support approaches.

Organizations applying should already be delivering therapeutic mental health services and demonstrate how their work supports young women in meaningful and accessible ways.

Geographic Eligibility

Eligible organizations must operate within one or more of the following regions:

  • Northern Ireland
  • Scotland
  • North East England
  • North West England
  • Yorkshire and Humber
  • The Midlands

Additionally, organizations operating across the UK or throughout England are also eligible to apply if the proposed work aligns with the programme’s geographic priorities.

Target Age Group

Organizations must work with young people aged 14–25.

Applicants may support wider age ranges overall, but the funded work must clearly include services for girls and young women within the eligible age category.

Why This Funding Programme Matters

Mental health concerns among young women continue to rise across the UK, with increasing pressure on community organizations and therapeutic support services.

Young Women in Mind 2026–2028 aims to tackle these challenges by investing directly in organizations already making a difference in their communities.

The programme recognizes that:

  • Early intervention is critical
  • Gender-specific support improves outcomes
  • Community-based services increase accessibility
  • Long-term investment strengthens sustainability

By supporting charities with flexible and meaningful funding, the programme seeks to prevent mental health challenges from becoming deeply entrenched while improving access to trusted therapeutic care.

Key Principles of the Programme

The funding initiative emphasizes:

  • Trauma-informed support
  • Community trust and accessibility
  • Gender-responsive care
  • Therapeutic quality and effectiveness
  • Long-term mental health outcomes
  • Learning and evaluation

Organizations are encouraged to review the programme’s official guidance materials to better understand the principles shaping the funding priorities.

Application Process

Interested organizations should:

  1. Review the official funding guidelines
  2. Complete the eligibility quiz
  3. Prepare and submit a stage one application
  4. If shortlisted, proceed to stage two

Applicants are encouraged to carefully assess eligibility before applying.

Important Dates

Programme Opening Date

  • 11 May 2026

Stage One Application Deadline

  • 22 June 2026

Stage Two Application Deadline

  • 24 August 2026

Notification of Decisions

  • End of November 2026

Organizations are advised to begin preparing applications early due to the competitive nature of the programme.

For more information click here 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post Young Women in Mind 2026–2028 Grant Programme: Up to £500,000 for Mental Health Services Supporting Young Women in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: 2026–2027 NABJ-Apple News Fellowship: Paid Journalism Fellowship at Apple News for Emerging Digital Journalists
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The 2026–2027 NABJ-Apple News Fellowship is now open for applications, offering an extraordinary opportunity for recent graduates and early-career journalists to gain hands-on experience at one of the world’s leading digital news platforms. Through a partnership between Apple News and the National Association of Black Journalists (NABJ), this fellowship is designed to help emerging journalists […]

The post 2026–2027 NABJ-Apple News Fellowship: Paid Journalism Fellowship at Apple News for Emerging Digital Journalists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The 2026–2027 NABJ-Apple News Fellowship is now open for applications, offering an extraordinary opportunity for recent graduates and early-career journalists to gain hands-on experience at one of the world’s leading digital news platforms. Through a partnership between Apple News and the National Association of Black Journalists (NABJ), this fellowship is designed to help emerging journalists build practical newsroom expertise while working at the intersection of journalism, media innovation, and technology.

The fellowship provides participants with direct exposure to Apple News editorial operations, collaboration with major publisher partners, mentorship from experienced newsroom professionals, and competitive compensation throughout the program period.

This unique journalism fellowship is ideal for aspiring digital journalists seeking newsroom experience in breaking news coverage, editorial curation, audience engagement, newsletters, and audio storytelling.

About the NABJ-Apple News Fellowship

Apple News reaches millions of readers worldwide by delivering trusted journalism from globally recognized news organizations. Through this fellowship, Apple and NABJ aim to create pathways for emerging journalism talent to gain experience in digital media production and editorial decision-making at a world-class newsroom.

The selected fellow will join the Apple News editorial team and participate in a structured learning experience that introduces them to the platform’s major editorial divisions. Fellows will work alongside Apple editors and publishing partners while contributing directly to editorial projects and news coverage strategies.

The fellowship combines professional newsroom training, mentorship, editorial collaboration, and practical experience in modern digital journalism.

Fellowship Structure and Training Experience

The NABJ-Apple News Fellowship is carefully structured to provide broad editorial exposure before allowing fellows to specialize in a key area of interest.

Editorial Rotations

During the first three months, the fellow will rotate through multiple editorial teams within Apple News. These rotations are intended to help participants understand the platform’s editorial ecosystem and develop a versatile journalism skill set.

Editorial areas may include:

  • Breaking news coverage
  • Audio journalism
  • Newsletter production
  • Editorial curation
  • Audience engagement
  • News planning and coordination

These rotations allow fellows to explore multiple newsroom functions while working with experienced editorial professionals.

Specialization Phase

After completing the initial rotations, the fellow and Apple News leadership will jointly determine a specialized editorial focus area for the remaining six months of the program.

This second phase allows fellows to deepen their expertise and contribute more strategically to Apple News operations.

Fellowship Responsibilities

Participants in the NABJ-Apple News Fellowship will play an active role within the newsroom and contribute directly to editorial workflows.

Key responsibilities include:

Editorial Curation

Fellows will curate high-quality news content while maintaining strong editorial standards related to accuracy, relevance, and context.

Writing and Display Copy

The selected candidate will write engaging display copy that encourages audience engagement with stories highlighted by Apple News and publisher partners.

News Event Coverage Planning

Fellows will help develop editorial plans for major global and national news events, contributing to newsroom coordination and coverage strategies.

Collaboration with Publisher Partners

The fellowship includes direct communication and collaboration with editors from Apple News publisher partners, helping fellows build industry relationships and editorial coordination skills.

Standards and Quality Assurance

Participants will work with the News Standards team to ensure all editorial content meets Apple News curation standards and editorial policies.

Metrics and Strategic Growth

Fellows will learn how editorial performance metrics influence newsroom decisions and audience engagement strategies.

Internal Communication and Presentations

Participants will communicate their work internally and present project findings and editorial insights to the broader Apple News organization.

Fellowship Benefits and Compensation

The 2026–2027 NABJ-Apple News Fellowship offers substantial professional and financial benefits.

Competitive Pay

  • Compensation: $45 per hour
  • Work schedule: Up to 40 hours per week

This makes the fellowship one of the more competitive paid journalism fellowships available to recent graduates and early-career reporters.

Mentorship and Career Development

The selected fellow will receive:

  • Weekly one-on-one meetings with their manager
  • Dedicated mentorship support
  • Continuous editorial feedback
  • Professional development opportunities

Hands-On Experience at Apple News

Fellows gain direct experience inside one of the most influential digital news ecosystems in the world.

Hybrid and Remote Flexibility

Apple operates in a hybrid work environment. However, remote applicants will still be considered, creating greater accessibility for qualified candidates.

Fellowship Duration

The NABJ-Apple News Fellowship runs from:

  • August 2026 to May 2027

Program timeline:

  • Three months of editorial rotations
  • Six months of specialization in a selected editorial area

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must meet all eligibility requirements to be considered.

NABJ Membership Requirement

Candidates must:

  • Be current NABJ members in good standing during the application period and throughout the fellowship

Work Authorization

Applicants must:

  • Be legally permitted to work in the United States
  • Obtain all necessary visas and employment authorization before the fellowship begins

Academic and Professional Qualifications

Eligible applicants must:

  • Graduate by June 2026 from an accredited U.S. college or university, OR
  • Have fewer than three years of journalism experience

Applicants must also have:

  • A cumulative GPA of 3.0 or higher on a 4.0 scale
  • A degree in journalism, communications, or a related field, OR
  • Prior experience with digital or print publications, including student media

Journalism Commitment

Candidates should demonstrate:

  • Strong interest in editing and curation
  • Excellent news judgment
  • Passion for digital journalism
  • Commitment to a journalism career

Why This Fellowship Matters

The journalism industry is rapidly evolving, and digital storytelling now plays a central role in how audiences consume information. The NABJ-Apple News Fellowship provides emerging journalists with the opportunity to develop critical newsroom skills inside a globally recognized media platform.

Participants gain exposure not only to editorial workflows, but also to audience engagement, platform strategy, multimedia journalism, and newsroom collaboration in the digital age.

For aspiring journalists seeking meaningful newsroom experience and professional mentorship, this fellowship can serve as a major career accelerator.

Application Process

To apply for the 2026–2027 NABJ-Apple News Fellowship, applicants must complete the official application form and ensure they meet all fellowship requirements before submission.

Applicants should prepare:

  • Academic transcripts
  • Proof of NABJ membership
  • Journalism portfolio or publication samples
  • Resume or CV
  • Any additional required application materials

Application Deadline

No official application deadline has been stated in the provided announcement. Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible once applications are open.

For more information click here 

APPLY HERE 

Discover more opportunities here 

The post 2026–2027 NABJ-Apple News Fellowship: Paid Journalism Fellowship at Apple News for Emerging Digital Journalists appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Independent Expert Group for the ARISE Program (CIF): Global Call for Climate Finance and Resilience Experts
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
The Climate Investment Funds (CIF), established in 2008, is one of the world’s leading multilateral climate finance initiatives supporting developing countries in their transition toward low-emission, climate-resilient development. Through its collaboration with multilateral development banks (MDBs) and partner governments, CIF has pioneered large-scale concessional financing aimed at driving systemic change in energy, infrastructure, agriculture, water, […]

The post Independent Expert Group for the ARISE Program (CIF): Global Call for Climate Finance and Resilience Experts appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Climate Investment Funds (CIF), established in 2008, is one of the world’s leading multilateral climate finance initiatives supporting developing countries in their transition toward low-emission, climate-resilient development. Through its collaboration with multilateral development banks (MDBs) and partner governments, CIF has pioneered large-scale concessional financing aimed at driving systemic change in energy, infrastructure, agriculture, water, and social resilience systems.

Building on 17 years of implementation experience under the Pilot Program for Climate Resilience (PPCR), CIF has launched a transformative initiative known as the Accelerating Resilience Investments and Innovations for Sustainable Economies (ARISE) program. ARISE is designed to help developing countries convert national resilience priorities into structured, investable, and scalable climate solutions.

To support the technical integrity, fairness, and strategic direction of this program, CIF is now recruiting an Independent Expert Group (IEG) composed of globally recognized professionals in climate change, finance, and development economics.

A critical milestone in this initiative is the submission of Expressions of Interest (EoIs) by participating countries, with a deadline set for June 8th, 2026.

About the ARISE Program

The ARISE program is a forward-looking climate resilience financing framework that focuses on turning climate adaptation needs into actionable investment pipelines.

It prioritizes critical systems that are most vulnerable to climate risks, including:

  1. Food systems and agricultural resilience
  2. Water security and resource management
  3. Climate-resilient infrastructure development
  4. Sustainable and low-carbon energy systems

The program seeks to ensure that climate finance is not only accessible but also strategically deployed to generate long-term resilience outcomes and unlock private sector participation.

Role of the Independent Expert Group (IEG)

The Independent Expert Group (IEG) will consist of six internationally selected experts with deep technical and strategic experience in climate adaptation, development finance, and economic systems transformation.

The group will include balanced representation from both contributing and recipient countries, ensuring fairness, inclusivity, and technical rigor in decision-making. From among the six members, two co-chairs will be appointed.

Key Responsibilities

The Independent Expert Group will undertake the following core functions:

1. Evaluation of Country Submissions

  • Review Expressions of Interest (EoIs) submitted by eligible countries
  • Assess proposals against established CIF and ARISE evaluation criteria
  • Ensure alignment with resilience investment priorities

2. Technical Research and Review

  • Analyze annexed project and policy documents
  • Conduct supplementary desk research using the Integrated Results Framework (IRF)
  • Validate technical feasibility and investment readiness

3. Stakeholder Consultation

  • Conduct structured interviews with country representatives
  • Engage with multilateral development banks (MDBs)
  • Confirm compliance with MDB partnership standards and coordination frameworks

4. Reporting and Recommendations

  • Produce a ranked list of countries based on evaluation outcomes
  • Submit formal recommendations to the Strategic Climate Fund (SCF) Trust Fund Committee (TFC)
  • Provide methodological notes detailing evaluation approaches
  • Document limitations, challenges, and gaps identified during assessment
  • Include a full list of consulted stakeholders for transparency and accountability

Selection Criteria for Applicants

Applicants interested in serving on the Independent Expert Group must meet the following requirements:

Academic Qualifications

  • A Master’s degree or PhD in fields relevant to climate change, development economics, environmental science, finance, or related disciplines

Professional Experience

  • Minimum of 12 years of relevant professional experience
  • Proven work in climate resilience, development finance, or related sectors
  • Experience in developing country contexts is highly preferred

Additional Preferred Qualifications

  • Prior experience working with multilateral institutions, international organizations, or global development agencies is an advantage but not mandatory

Timeline and Availability

Selected experts must be available for engagement between June and October 2026.

  • Primary workload duration: approximately 6 to 8 weeks
  • Work will include review cycles, consultations, and report drafting phases
  • Flexibility and responsiveness during peak evaluation periods will be essential

Application Process and Requirements

Applicants are required to submit their applications directly via email:

CIF Secretariat Email: CIFSecretariat@cif.org

Application Requirements:

  • Full name and contact information
  • Detailed area of expertise
  • Updated curriculum vitae (CV/resume)
  • Disclosure of any conflicts of interest (including current or past employment with CIF partner MDBs or related institutions)

Important Deadline:

  • Application submission deadline: May 21st, 2026
  • Submissions through any channel other than the official email will not be considered

Compensation

Selected experts will be remunerated in accordance with World Bank consultant procurement rules and regulations, ensuring standardized and competitive international compensation structures for technical experts.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Consultancy Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Independent Expert Group for the ARISE Program (CIF): Global Call for Climate Finance and Resilience Experts appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: RISE Together Fund 2026 Open Call: Grants for BAMEMSA Community Power Building Organizations
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the RISE Together Fund (RTF) 2026 Open Call, a grant opportunity hosted by Proteus Fund to support organizations building community power and advancing justice for BAMEMSA communities. RISE Together Fund invests in organizations led by and serving Black, African, Arab, Middle Eastern, Muslim, and South Asian (BAMEMSA) communities. The fund […]

The post RISE Together Fund 2026 Open Call: Grants for BAMEMSA Community Power Building Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the RISE Together Fund (RTF) 2026 Open Call, a grant opportunity hosted by Proteus Fund to support organizations building community power and advancing justice for BAMEMSA communities.

RISE Together Fund invests in organizations led by and serving Black, African, Arab, Middle Eastern, Muslim, and South Asian (BAMEMSA) communities. The fund supports movement-building efforts that use multiple strategies to create lasting social change rooted in community leadership and collective power.

This opportunity is designed to strengthen organizations working at the intersection of racial justice, immigrant rights, faith-based organizing, civic engagement, and community empowerment.

About the RISE Together Fund

The RISE Together Fund provides resources to organizations that center the voices and leadership of BAMEMSA communities. The fund recognizes that meaningful change requires diverse approaches, including advocacy, organizing, narrative change, coalition building, and leadership development.

By investing in community power, the fund aims to advance equity, justice, and long-term systemic transformation.

What the Fund Supports

Eligible organizations may engage in activities such as:

  • Community organizing and movement building
  • Civic engagement and voter participation
  • Leadership development
  • Narrative and cultural change
  • Policy advocacy
  • Coalition building
  • Racial and social justice initiatives

Funding Benefits

Selected organizations will receive grant support to strengthen and expand their community-driven work.

Specific grant amounts and detailed funding terms are outlined in the official Request for Proposals (RFP).

Who Should Apply

This opportunity is intended for community power-building organizations that:

  • Are rooted in BAMEMSA communities
  • Use multiple strategies to create social change
  • Center community leadership and grassroots organizing
  • Advance justice and equity

Geographic Eligibility

Applicants should review the official RFP for location and eligibility details, as specific geographic requirements may apply.

Application Deadline

Applications must be submitted by:

15 June 2026

Apply Here

For more Grant opportunities, Click here

The post RISE Together Fund 2026 Open Call: Grants for BAMEMSA Community Power Building Organizations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: NASA Service Member Fellowships 2026: Fully Immersive Opportunities for Transitioning U.S. Military Personnel Through SkillBridge & Operation Warfighter
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) is offering an extraordinary opportunity for transitioning U.S. military personnel through its Service Member Fellowships Program. Conducted in partnership with the Department of Defense (DoD) SkillBridge Program and the Defense Health Agency (DHA) Operation Warfighter (OWF) Program, these fellowships allow active-duty service members to gain meaningful, mission-driven experience […]

The post NASA Service Member Fellowships 2026: Fully Immersive Opportunities for Transitioning U.S. Military Personnel Through SkillBridge & Operation Warfighter appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) is offering an extraordinary opportunity for transitioning U.S. military personnel through its Service Member Fellowships Program. Conducted in partnership with the Department of Defense (DoD) SkillBridge Program and the Defense Health Agency (DHA) Operation Warfighter (OWF) Program, these fellowships allow active-duty service members to gain meaningful, mission-driven experience at one of the world’s most respected space and research agencies.

Whether your background is in engineering, cybersecurity, public affairs, human resources, science, technology, administration, or program management, NASA’s fellowship pathways are designed to help military personnel successfully transition into civilian careers while contributing to real NASA missions.

The fellowship opportunities are available across multiple NASA centers nationwide, including remote and hybrid roles.

Applicants selected for the program may work alongside NASA professionals in areas connected to:

  • Human spaceflight
  • Scientific research
  • Technology development
  • Communications and outreach
  • Program management
  • Cybersecurity and IT
  • Engineering and innovation
  • Administrative operations
  • Public affairs and strategic communications

This program is not only a career-transition opportunity but also a chance to become part of the NASA ecosystem and contribute to projects that shape the future of exploration and innovation.

About the NASA Service Member Fellowships Program

NASA’s Service Member Fellowships Program provides active-duty military personnel nearing separation from service the opportunity to work directly with NASA during their final months of military duty.

The agency currently participates in two major military transition programs:

  1. SkillBridge Fellowship Program
  2. Operation Warfighter (OWF) Fellowship Program

Through these pathways, selected participants can work with NASA for up to 180 days before completing military service.

NASA does not advertise individual vacancies ahead of time. Instead, applicants submit their profiles into a centralized candidate pool. NASA centers then review applications and match candidates with opportunities based on mission needs and candidate qualifications.

Current Fellowship Opportunities at NASA

NASA has announced several exciting openings and pathways across the agency.

Johnson Space Center (JSC)

Opportunities at NASA’s Johnson Space Center include:

Engineering Roles

These positions require:

  • A master’s degree
  • Relevant professional or technical experience
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities

Engineering fellows may support:

  • Spaceflight systems
  • Research and development
  • Aerospace technologies
  • Mission operations
  • Technical innovation initiatives

Public Affairs Roles

Public affairs fellows may contribute to:

  • NASA communications campaigns
  • Outreach programs
  • Media engagement
  • Digital storytelling
  • Strategic messaging and content creation

These positions are ideal for individuals with communication, journalism, media production, branding, or outreach experience.

NASA Headquarters (Remote Opportunities)

NASA Headquarters is also recruiting fellows for remote program management support roles.

Responsibilities may include:

  • Supporting implementation of the NASA Service Member Fellowships Program
  • Coordinating agency-wide initiatives
  • Assisting with operational management
  • Supporting strategic planning and reporting
  • Engaging with stakeholders across NASA centers

Remote opportunities provide flexibility while allowing fellows to contribute to important NASA-wide operations.

Why This Fellowship Matters

Transitioning from military to civilian life can be challenging, particularly when trying to align military experience with civilian careers. NASA’s fellowship program helps bridge that gap by giving service members practical, high-impact experience inside a federal agency environment.

Participants gain:

  • Hands-on NASA work experience
  • Exposure to federal workplace culture
  • Professional networking opportunities
  • Career development support
  • Resume enhancement as a “NASA Fellow”
  • Access to mission-driven projects
  • Mentorship from NASA professionals

Although participation does not guarantee future employment with NASA, the experience significantly strengthens candidates for future federal and private-sector careers.

Fields and Career Areas Available

NASA offers fellowships across a broad range of disciplines.

Possible fellowship areas include:

Engineering, Science & Technology

  • Aerospace Engineering
  • Mechanical Engineering
  • Systems Engineering
  • Physical Sciences
  • Research & Development

Information Technology & Cybersecurity

  • Cybersecurity Operations
  • Network Administration
  • IT Infrastructure
  • Software Support
  • Information Systems

Communications & Public Affairs

  • Media Relations
  • Public Communication
  • Content Development
  • Branding & Outreach
  • Strategic Communications

Business & Administration

  • Human Resources
  • Administration
  • Program Management
  • Financial Management
  • Procurement & Contracts
  • Budgeting & Accounting

Additional Fields

  • Safety & Occupational Health
  • Social Sciences
  • Mathematics & Statistics
  • Operational Analysis

Fellowship Duration

Selected fellows may participate during their final:

  • Minimum: 90 days
  • Maximum: 180 days

The fellowship occurs while participants remain on active duty status.

Locations and Work Environment

NASA fellowship opportunities are available:

  • Across multiple NASA centers in the United States
  • In hybrid work environments
  • Through fully remote positions in some cases

NASA facilities support various mission areas, so applicants are encouraged to research which centers align best with their professional interests.

Eligibility Requirements

SkillBridge Fellowship Eligibility

Applicants must:

  1. Be within the final 180 days of active-duty military service
  2. Be available for at least 90 days
  3. Complete the Transition Assistance Program (TAP)
  4. Obtain command approval
  5. Follow all service-specific SkillBridge regulations
  6. Understand that participation may be terminated due to mission requirements

Operation Warfighter (OWF) Eligibility

Applicants must:

  1. Be actively enrolled in the DHA Operation Warfighter Program
  2. Be assigned to a Regional OWF Coordinator
  3. Obtain medical and command approvals
  4. Follow the DHA Comprehensive Recovery Plan
  5. Acknowledge that participation may be terminated by medical providers or military command when necessary

Important Application Information

NASA independently manages participation in both SkillBridge and OWF fellowships.

SkillBridge Application Process

Step 1: Obtain Military Approval

Applicants should:

  • Review their branch’s SkillBridge policies
  • Coordinate with their base education center
  • Ensure eligibility for participation

Step 2: Research NASA Facilities

Applicants are encouraged to:

  • Explore NASA centers
  • Identify career-aligned mission areas
  • Understand available work environments

Step 3: Submit Application

Applicants must submit information through the official NASA Service Member Fellowship application platform.

Important note:
NASA does not publicly post fellowship vacancies beforehand. Applications remain in a candidate pool until sponsoring centers identify suitable candidates.

Operation Warfighter Application Process

Step 1: Connect with Regional Coordinator

Applicants should:

  • Contact their assigned OWF Coordinator
  • Confirm eligibility and program alignment

Step 2: Submit Application

Candidates must apply through NASA’s Veterans Application platform.

As with SkillBridge, NASA centers review applications internally and contact selected candidates directly.

NASA’s Policy on Artificial Intelligence in Applications

NASA explicitly prohibits the use of artificial intelligence tools to draft fellowship application narratives.

Applicants must:

  • Write all responses independently
  • Submit original materials
  • Avoid AI-assisted writing tools

NASA may verify submissions, and failure to comply can result in disqualification.

Veterans Careers at NASA

NASA continues to support veterans and military spouses through:

  • Career development opportunities
  • Flexible workplace arrangements
  • Veteran-focused hiring pathways
  • Federal hiring preferences

Veterans separated under honorable conditions may qualify for additional hiring authorities and employment advantages within the federal system.

Fellowship Benefits

Participants may benefit from:

  • Direct NASA work experience
  • Exposure to high-impact national projects
  • Professional mentorship
  • Career transition support
  • Enhanced federal employment readiness
  • Networking with NASA teams
  • Resume credibility as a NASA Fellow
  • Participation in mission-driven work environments

Deadline

NASA has not announced a fixed application deadline.

Applications are accepted on a rolling basis and maintained within a candidate pool for review by NASA centers.

Interested applicants are strongly encouraged to apply as early as possible before entering their final 180 days of active-duty service.

Final Thoughts

NASA’s Service Member Fellowships Program is one of the most unique military transition opportunities currently available in the United States. It combines professional development, national service, technical innovation, and real-world experience within a globally respected organization.

For transitioning service members interested in engineering, public affairs, technology, administration, or scientific exploration, this fellowship offers a rare opportunity to work directly with NASA while preparing for the next phase of civilian life.

Applicants who meet the eligibility requirements should begin preparing early, coordinate with their military leadership, and submit strong, original applications aligned with NASA’s mission and values.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can apply through the official application portal HERE.

Click HERE for more information about the NASA Service Member Fellowships Program.

Explore more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here.

The post NASA Service Member Fellowships 2026: Fully Immersive Opportunities for Transitioning U.S. Military Personnel Through SkillBridge & Operation Warfighter appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
